name: Sendero Circular de Lanjarón - trail running (PR-A-345)
name_original: Sendero Circular de Lanjarón – trail running (PR-A-345)
region_intro: Jogging around Lanjarón offers diverse landscapes at the foothills of the Sierra Nevada mountains. The region features varied elevations, from lush pine forests to ancient chestnut and almond groves. Many running routes incorporate historical Moorish irrigation channels, known as acequias, and follow rivers fed by melting snow. Higher trails provide expansive views across the Lecrín Valley. Best jogging routes around Lanjarón The most popular jogging route is Bordaila - Acequia Nueva Trail, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 31 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Sendero Circular de Lanjarón - trail running (PR-A-345), a difficult 7.7 miles (12.3 km) path. This circular route offers panoramic views of the Sierra Nevada foothills. Local runners also love the Hermitage of the Tajo de la Cruz – Lanjarón River loop from Lanjarón, a 11.4 miles (18.4 km) trail leading through river valleys and past historical points of interest, often completed in about 2 hours 51 minutes. Jogging around Lanjarón is defined by Sierra Nevada foothills, ancient acequias, and lush river valleys. The network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging trail runs. The routes in Lanjarón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.7 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 800 runners have used komoot to explore Lanjarón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes around Lanjarón?
A: Running routes around Lanjarón offer incredibly diverse terrain, from lush pine forests and ancient chestnut groves to terraced farms. You'll encounter varied elevations, with many trails featuring significant ascents and descents as they traverse the foothills of the Sierra Nevada. Historical Moorish irrigation channels, known as &lt;em&gt;acequias&lt;/em&gt;, are also a common and scenic feature.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Lanjarón?
A: While many routes in Lanjarón are rated as difficult due to significant elevation changes, there are also moderate options available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19347512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Placeta de Santa Ana – Lanjarón loop from Lanjarón&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.8 km route with less elevation gain, making it a good choice for those looking for a less strenuous run.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while jogging in Lanjarón?
A: Lanjarón's running routes are rich with natural beauty. You can explore areas like Parque el Salao and Parque de la Encina with their well-maintained paths and ancient trees. Many trails follow the Río Lanjarón valley, offering views of towering cliffs and lush environments. You might also encounter the famous &lt;em&gt;acequias&lt;/em&gt;, ancient irrigation channels, and on clear days, higher trails provide breathtaking vistas across the Lecrín Valley and even to the Mediterranean Sea.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Lanjarón area?
A: Yes, Lanjarón offers several excellent circular running routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986575024/sendero-circular-de-lajaron-trail-running-pr-a-345" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero Circular de Lanjarón - trail running (PR-A-345)&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 12.3 km path that provides panoramic views of the Sierra Nevada foothills. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14598240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Tajo de la Cruz – Lanjarón River loop from Lanjarón&lt;/a&gt;, which is a longer 18.4 km route.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Lanjarón?
A: The running routes in Lanjarón are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of &lt;strong&gt;3.7 out of 5 stars&lt;/strong&gt; from over 10 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse landscapes, the challenge of the varied elevations, and the scenic beauty of the ancient &lt;em&gt;acequias&lt;/em&gt; and river valleys.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by historical sites or landmarks?
A: Absolutely. Many running routes in Lanjarón incorporate historical elements. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14598240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Tajo de la Cruz – Lanjarón River loop from Lanjarón&lt;/a&gt; leads past historical points of interest. You can also find trails that offer views of the ruins of the old Moorish castle or pass by small rural churches (&lt;em&gt;ermitas&lt;/em&gt;) like Ermita del Tajo de la Cruz.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls or gorges?
A: Yes, the region around Lanjarón is known for its dramatic natural features. While jogging, you might encounter stunning gorges such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3823223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de la Luna Gorge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4059982" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Salado Ravine&lt;/a&gt;. There are also beautiful waterfalls like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4203280" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cahorros de Nigüelas Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajo de Cortés Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; that can be incorporated into your running adventures.

Q: How many running routes are available in Lanjarón on komoot?
A: There are over 10 dedicated running routes around Lanjarón listed on komoot, offering a range of experiences from moderate paths to challenging trail runs. This includes 3 moderate routes and 9 difficult routes, ensuring options for various fitness levels.

Q: What is a challenging long-distance running route in Lanjarón?
A: For experienced runners seeking a significant challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33888577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acequia Montalbán – Lanjarón River loop from Lanjarón&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option. This difficult route spans approximately 21 km and features over 1100 meters of elevation gain, offering a demanding and rewarding trail running experience.

Q: Are there any caves I can explore near the running trails?
A: Yes, the area around Lanjarón features several interesting caves. While jogging, you might be near highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5453047" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Riscos&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3336310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Doves&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2086363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Secret Cave&lt;/a&gt;. These can add an extra element of exploration to your outdoor activity.

Q: What is the best time of year for trail running in Lanjarón?
A: Lanjarón, situated at the foothills of the Sierra Nevada, offers good running conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures for trail running. In summer, it's advisable to run early in the morning or late in the afternoon to avoid the midday heat. Winter can be beautiful, but higher trails may have snow, requiring appropriate gear and caution.
tips: This hermitage was built in the place where a cross had been placed at the end of the 18th century to ward off supposed practices of witchcraft in the area. The cross was later replaced by another and finally the hermitage was built on the spot.
This is undoubtedly one of the best and most popular viewpoints to observe Lanjarón in the distance.

Lanjarón Castle, also known as the Moorish Castle, is a medieval fortress located in the town of Lanjarón, in the province of Granada. It is located about 400 metres from the centre of Lanjarón, on a rocky promontory overlooking the valley of the Lanjarón River. 
Built between the 13th and 16th centuries, during the Nasrid and Christian periods, it was part of the defensive programme of the sultans Yusuf I and Mohammad V. Although in ruins, the castle has been protected and restored on several occasions. Since 1993, it has been listed as a Site of Cultural Interest.
